meta.name: Dal Tadka (Everyday Indian Lentils)
meta.subtitle: Creamy lentils finished with a sizzling cumin-garlic tempering

source.name: SSA canonical cuisines/indian/recipes/dal.json
notes: Canonical dal baseline with a separate tadka step (tempering) for identity.
Designed to be deterministic + adaptable across lentil types (red, toor, moong).
Pressure cooker / Instant Pot and stovetop paths supported via variations.

summary: Dal Tadka is the Indian weeknight backbone: soft lentils simmered with aromatics, then finished with a hot tadka (tempering) of cumin, garlic, and chili for big flavor. Great with basmati rice or roti.

yield.notes: Serves 6 as a main with rice/roti, or 8 as a side.

optional.label: Immersion blender
optional.notes: Optional for extra creamy dal; pulse briefly.

signals.freezerFriendly: yes (freeze dal base; add fresh tadka after reheating for best aroma)

notes: Naturally vegetarian; vegan if using oil instead of ghee/butter.
Gluten-free by default; confirm asafoetida (hing) source if used (some contain wheat).

ingredients.label: Red lentils (masoor dal), rinsed
notes: Red lentils cook quickly and naturally break down into a creamy texture.
Substitutions: toor dal or moong dal—see variations for time adjustments.

ingredients.label: Water
notes: Add more during simmer if needed; dal should be spoonable and silky.
If you prefer thicker dal, start with 5 cups.

ingredients.label: Garlic, minced (base)
ingredients.notes: Additional garlic is used in the tadka.

ingredients.label: Tomato, diced (optional but common)
notes: Adds brightness and body; you can omit for a simpler dal.
Substitute: 1/2 cup canned diced tomatoes.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Start with 1.5 tsp and adjust after simmering.

ingredients.label: Ghee (or oil) for tadka
notes: Ghee is classic. For vegan, use neutral oil or coconut oil.
If using butter, keep heat moderate to avoid burning milk solids.

ingredients.label: Mustard seeds (optional)
ingredients.notes: Common in South Indian leaning dal; optional here.

ingredients.label: Dried red chili (whole)
ingredients.notes: Use 0–2 depending on heat preference. Break for more heat.

ingredients.label: Curry leaves (optional)
ingredients.notes: Adds an unmistakable aroma; omit if unavailable.

ingredients.label: Lemon wedges (optional finish)
ingredients.notes: A squeeze at the end brightens the pot.

steps.title: Rinse the lentils
instructions: Place lentils in a fine mesh strainer and rinse under cool water until the water runs mostly clear.
checks: Rinsing reduces foam and keeps flavor clean.

steps.title: Sweat aromatics (optional but adds depth)
instructions: In a pot over medium heat, add 1 tbsp ghee/oil (taken from the tadka amount if desired).
Add chopped onion and cook 4–6 minutes until soft.
Add minced garlic and ginger; cook 45–60 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in turmeric for 15 seconds to bloom.

steps.title: Simmer the dal until creamy
instructions: Add rinsed lentils, water, diced tomato (if using), and 1.5 tsp salt.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a steady simmer.
Simmer 20–30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until lentils are very soft and the dal is creamy.
If it gets too thick, add water 1/4 cup at a time. If too thin, simmer uncovered a few minutes longer.
checks: Red lentils should break down; texture should be spoonable and silky.
Skim foam if needed during the first 5 minutes.

steps.title: Adjust texture and seasoning
instructions: For extra creamy texture, mash a portion of the lentils with the back of a spoon, or briefly pulse with an immersion blender (optional).
Keep the dal warm on low while you make the tadka.
checks: Dal thickens as it sits—aim slightly looser than your final target.

steps.title: Make the tadka (tempering)
instructions: In a small pan over medium-high heat, melt ghee (or heat oil).
Add cumin seeds (and mustard seeds if using). Let sizzle 15–30 seconds until fragrant; mustard will pop.
Add sliced garlic and dried chilies; cook 30–60 seconds until garlic is just turning golden (do not burn).
Add curry leaves if using; they will crackle.
checks: This step is fast—have everything ready.
Golden garlic is perfect; burnt garlic makes the whole pot bitter.

steps.title: Finish the dal with tadka and garnish
instructions: Immediately pour the hot tadka over the dal (it should sizzle).
Stir gently to distribute (or leave some on top for presentation).
Finish with cilantro and a squeeze of lemon if desired.
checks: Adding tadka at the end preserves aroma.
If serving later, make tadka fresh right before serving for best flavor.

notes: Dal thickens in the fridge; loosen with water when reheating.
Reheat gently; stir well to prevent scorching.
Freeze dal base in portions.
For best aroma, make tadka fresh after reheating.
Cool leftovers quickly and refrigerate within 2 hours.
Reheat until steaming hot; stir frequently to avoid scorching.
Use 1.5 cups toor dal and 7 cups water.
Simmer time increases to ~40–55 minutes on stovetop; stir more often near the end.
Pressure cooker: 10–12 minutes at pressure with natural release.
Use split moong dal; simmer ~25–35 minutes.
Add a pinch of asafoetida (hing) in the tadka if desired.
Sauté aromatics, then add lentils + water + turmeric + salt.
Cook at high pressure: red lentils 6 minutes, toor/moong 10 minutes. Natural release 10 minutes.
Make tadka separately and pour on top at the end.
Use neutral oil or coconut oil for tadka.
Finish with lemon for brightness.
Add 1/2 tsp Kashmiri chili powder to the simmering dal or include a fresh green chili in the base.
Increase dried red chilies in tadka to 2–3.
